# Project Architecture

This is an enterprise Go service generated by [goplate](https://github.com/cccvno1/goplate).
Follow these rules for all code changes.

## goplate CLI

This project was scaffolded by `goplate`. The CLI is available in your PATH and outputs
structured JSON. Use it when you need to understand the project's extension system or
available infrastructure:

```bash
goplate info                      # Build info, version, env vars
goplate extensions list           # All available extensions
goplate extensions show <name>    # Full detail: marker, files, deps, config domain
goplate examples list             # Preset extension combinations
goplate schema                    # Machine-readable spec of all commands and flags
```

When implementing infrastructure that goplate supports (HTTP, gRPC, Postgres, Redis,
Kafka, Worker), check the extension details first:

```bash
goplate extensions show postgres  # See owned files, config domain, go_deps
```

## Local Development Workflow

Before writing any feature code, ensure infrastructure is running:

```bash
make docker-up          # Start all infra services (Postgres, Redis, Kafka, etc.)
docker compose ps       # Verify all services are healthy
```


Start the application:

```bash
make run                # Build and run with APP_ENV=local
```

Troubleshooting infrastructure:

```bash
docker compose logs <service>   # e.g. docker compose logs postgres
docker compose restart <service>
```


## Directory Responsibilities

| Directory | Purpose | May Import |
|-----------|---------|------------|
| `cmd/server/` | Entry point only | `internal/base/boot`, `internal/base/conf` |
| `internal/base/boot/` | Wiring + lifecycle orchestration | everything under `internal/` |
| `internal/base/conf/` | Config struct | pkg/* only |
| `internal/domain/` | Shared business types + error codes | pkg/* only |
| `internal/<feature>/` | One package per feature | `internal/domain/`, pkg/* |

## Hard Boundaries

1. **`internal/domain/`** must NEVER import any `internal/` package.
2. **Feature packages** must NEVER import other features or `internal/base/`.
3. **Only `internal/base/boot/`** may import feature packages (for wiring).
4. Framework types (`net/http`, `database/sql`) must NOT leak into `internal/domain/`.
5. All inter-feature communication goes through interface injection at wire time.

## Feature Package Structure

Each feature lives in `internal/<name>/` with these fixed files:

| File | Role |
|------|------|
| `model.go` | Domain model types — no framework imports |
| `service.go` | Business logic — `(ctx context.Context, in XxxInput) (*XxxOutput, error)` |
| `wire.go` | Feature assembly — called from `base/boot/boot.go` |

Additional files are driven by the feature's requirements, not by a template.
Common patterns when the corresponding infrastructure is available:

| `handler.go` | Parse HTTP request → call service → format response |
| `dto.go` | Request / Response / Input / Output DTOs |

Other files are named by role: `sender.go`, `indexer.go`, `client.go`, `worker.go`, etc.

## Error Model

- Business errors use `errkit.New(code, msg)` — codes defined in `internal/domain/errors.go`.
- Transport layers project `errkit.Code` → HTTP status / gRPC code. Never invent status codes.
- Wrap errors with context: `fmt.Errorf("feature: operation: %w", err)`.

## Config Conventions

- Config structs live in `internal/base/conf/conf.go`.
- Runtime config in `configs/base/*.yaml`, environment overrides in `configs/<env>/*.yaml`.
- Sensitive fields must come from environment variables, never from YAML.

## HTTP Conventions

- Routes registered in feature `wire.go` via `mux.HandleFunc`. `router.go` is for infrastructure routes (health check).
- Middleware chain defined in `internal/base/middleware/middleware.go`.
- Middleware injects request ID and trace ID into context via `reqctx` package.
- In handlers: `httpkit.Decode(r, &req)` to parse body, `httpkit.JSON(w, status, NewVerbResponse(out))` to respond, `httpkit.Error(w, err)` for errors.
- Always convert domain models to response DTOs before sending — never expose internal fields (password hashes, internal flags) to API consumers.
- Access request metadata in service via `r.Context()` — never pass framework types into service.


## Documentation

Maintain `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` when adding features or changing structure.
Record non-obvious decisions in `docs/decisions/`.
